module_name: Extend core capabilities of Microsoft Sustainability Manager

skills.definition: Configure and extend sustainability data tracking and reporting solutions for an organization's environmental, social, and governance metrics.
skills.evidence_quote: Extend core capabilities of Microsoft Sustainability Manager

skills.definition: Build and customize model-driven and low-code business applications on the Microsoft Power Platform.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Sustainability Manager is built on top of Microsoft Power Platform as a model-driven application. You get a set of low-code tools that can help your teams stay agile and build solutions

skills.definition: Build and extend dashboards and analytical reports using Microsoft Power BI.
skills.evidence_quote: you can customize your dashboards by using Microsoft Power BI extensions to suit your organizational operations

extraction.notes: Sustainability Manager is niche enough that ESG Reporting is the closest LinkedIn-style competency; Power Platform and Power BI are the underlying technical skills the module exercises.

module_name: Optimize your business process with process advisor

microsoft_surface_forms: process mining capability in Power Automate
skills.definition: Discover, map, and analyze existing business processes from observed activity to identify bottlenecks and automation opportunities.
skills.evidence_quote: The process mining capability in Power Automate helps make that task easier by providing a service that observes your actions and then documents the process for you. Additionally, process mining identifies bottlenecks and recommends automation solutions

skills.definition: Design and implement automated workflows that replicate human tasks across desktop and business applications.
skills.evidence_quote: The first step to automating a solution is knowing the process... process mining identifies bottlenecks and recommends automation solutions with a specific technology

skills.definition: Build automated workflows, desktop flows, and process recordings using Microsoft Power Automate.
skills.evidence_quote: With the process advisor capability in Power Automate, you can record existing business processes and analyze them

skills.definition: Analyze, map, and improve business processes by measuring completion times and identifying inefficiencies.
skills.evidence_quote: you can record existing business processes and analyze them to increase efficacy by tracking completion times and mapping various actions

extraction.notes: Power Automate and RPA overlap but represent the tool vs. the practice; kept both per default-to-include guidance.

module_name: Connect syslog data sources to Microsoft Sentinel

skills.definition: Deploy, configure, and operate Microsoft's cloud-native SIEM/SOAR platform to ingest and analyze security telemetry.
skills.evidence_quote: You send Syslog log data to the Microsoft Sentinel workspace using an Azure Monitor Agent Data Collection Rule (DCR)

skills.definition: Aggregate, correlate, and analyze security event data from disparate sources in a security information and event management platform.
skills.evidence_quote: You're a Security Operations Analyst working at a company that implemented Microsoft Sentinel. You need to collect log data from on-premises network appliances

skills.definition: Collect, forward, parse, and centralize log data from heterogeneous systems for analysis and retention.
skills.evidence_quote: Learn about the Azure Monitor Agent Linux Syslog Data Collection Rule configuration options, which enable you to parse Syslog data

skills.definition: Perform day-to-day monitoring, detection, and response activities in a security operations center.
skills.evidence_quote: You're a Security Operations Analyst working at a company that implemented Microsoft Sentinel

skills.definition: Extend Azure management and governance to servers and resources hosted outside of Azure.
skills.evidence_quote: install the Azure Connected Machine agent that enables you to manage your (Windows and) Linux machines hosted outside of Azure on your corporate network with Azure Arc

microsoft_surface_forms: Azure Monitor Syslog Data Collection Rule
skills.definition: Configure Azure Monitor agents and data collection rules to ingest telemetry from Azure and non-Azure resources.
skills.evidence_quote: you can install the Azure Monitor Linux Agent extension, and during that process you create an Azure Monitor Syslog Data Collection Rule

extraction.notes: Module is multi-disciplinary across SecOps/Sentinel and the Azure Arc/Monitor plumbing that enables ingestion; kept both layers.

module_name: Apply Networking concepts to Windows containers running on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and AKS Hybrid

skills.definition: Deploy, configure, and operate Kubernetes clusters and workloads, including networking and scaling.
skills.evidence_quote: developers often use a container management platform like **Kube[rnetes]

skills.definition: Provision and operate managed Kubernetes clusters on Azure and Azure-hybrid infrastructure.
skills.evidence_quote: Apply Networking concepts to Windows containers running on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and AKS Hybrid

skills.definition: Design and configure networking — load balancing, ingress, service connectivity — for containerized workloads.
skills.evidence_quote: Define networking configurations for Kubernetes environments and their pros and cons, then choose the best option for your scenario

skills.definition: Build, deploy, and operate containerized workloads that run on the Windows container runtime.
skills.evidence_quote: Apply Networking concepts to Windows containers running on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and AKS Hybrid

skills.definition: Package and run applications as containers, leveraging isolation and portability to deploy across environments.
skills.evidence_quote: Containers are a common development approach for microservice architectures and applications. A containers-based implementation uses hardware efficiently

skills.definition: Design applications as collections of independently deployable services that communicate over a network.
skills.evidence_quote: Containers are a common development approach for microservice architectures and applications

extraction.notes: Per guidance Windows Containers and Containerization can coexist; kept Microservices Architecture since it appears as the motivating design pattern.

module_name: Configure and deploy Teams Phone

skills.definition: Configure and administer Microsoft Teams features including calling, meetings, and policies for an organization.
skills.evidence_quote: Before Microsoft Teams Phone users can make or receive calls and use advanced features, phone numbers must be assigned and Microsoft Calling plans or Operator Connect must be configured.

skills.definition: Plan, deploy, and operate enterprise voice and conferencing services that integrate PSTN telephony with collaboration platforms.
skills.evidence_quote: Teams Phone includes the functionality to use Teams for calling to and from the traditional PSTN.

skills.definition: Configure voice-over-IP infrastructure including calling plans, dial plans, call routing policies, and caller ID for business telephony.
skills.evidence_quote: You'll then review key Teams Phone configuration options with calling policies, dial plans, call park policies, caller ID policies, and inbound call blocking

extraction.notes: PSTN, Operator Connect, Direct Routing, emergency calling and dial plans all fold up into VoIP Administration / Unified Communications per the rules.

module_name: Store local data with SQLite in a .NET MAUI app

skills.definition: Build cross-platform mobile and desktop applications using Microsoft's .NET Multi-platform App UI framework.
skills.evidence_quote: .NET Multi-platform App UI (MAUI) allows you to cache data locally on the device on which the application is running.

skills.definition: Design and build mobile applications that run on multiple device platforms from a shared codebase.
skills.evidence_quote: When you're building mobile applications, it's common to store data locally on a device for performance reasons.

skills.definition: Use the SQLite embedded relational database to persist and query structured data locally inside an application.
skills.evidence_quote: To store relational data, you can use a SQLite database.

skills.definition: Design local on-device data stores for offline-capable apps, including caching and synchronization patterns.
skills.evidence_quote: A more efficiently designed app downloads the user data once and saves it locally to the device.

skills.definition: Build applications on the .NET runtime using C# and Microsoft's application frameworks.
skills.evidence_quote: .NET Multi-platform App UI (MAUI) allows you to cache data locally on the device

extraction.notes: Included both .NET MAUI (the specific framework) and Cross-Platform Mobile Development (the discipline) since both coexist at the right granularity per rule 1 example of Windows Containers / Containerization.

module_name: Train and evaluate regression models

skills.definition: Apply statistical regression techniques to model relationships between variables and predict numeric outcomes.
skills.evidence_quote: Regression is a statistical technique of fundamental importance to science because of its ease of interpretation, robustness, and speed in calculation.

skills.definition: Build and train models that learn patterns from data to make predictions or decisions.
skills.evidence_quote: In machine learning, the goal of regression is to create a model that can predict a numeric, quantifiable value

skills.definition: Construct models from historical data to forecast future values or events, including evaluating predictive performance.
skills.evidence_quote: if a company that rents bicycles wants to predict the expected number of rentals on a given day in the future, a regression model can predict this number.

skills.definition: Assess machine learning model quality using appropriate metrics and validation techniques.
skills.evidence_quote: Train and evaluate regression models

extraction.notes: Evidence for Model Evaluation drawn from module title since the intro emphasizes prediction but the module name explicitly trains evaluation.

module_name: Enable patients to access the Patient Access portal

skills.definition: Configure and operate Microsoft's healthcare industry cloud solutions that surface patient health data, scheduling, and engagement to clinical staff and patients.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft for Healthcare includes Patient Access and Patient Service Center.

skills.definition: Design and deliver patient-facing digital experiences for self-service access to health information, appointment scheduling, and provider communication.
skills.evidence_quote: Provide patients with access to their health data, appointment scheduling, and information through the Patient Access portal

skills.definition: Build and configure Power Platform-based portals and model-driven applications to support business processes such as patient access.
skills.evidence_quote: Patients have a user-friendly portal to access their health information.

skills.definition: Implement chatbot experiences that handle automated user interactions and hand off to human agents when needed.
skills.evidence_quote: Patients can engage through automated chat conversations that hands off to your patient service center.

extraction.notes: Defaulted-to-include Conversational AI based on the health-bot/chat handoff reference in the intro.

module_name: Recurring contract billing in Dynamics 365 Finance

skills.definition: Configure and operate Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance modules to support core financial processes such as billing, invoicing, and revenue forecasting.
skills.evidence_quote: With recurring contract billing in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance, you can have more control of billing parameters at the line-item level.

skills.definition: Design and operate recurring billing schedules, contract renewals, and flexible pricing models for subscription-based revenue.
skills.evidence_quote: You might use recurring contract billing to prearrange a schedule to automatically charge a customer.

skills.definition: Use billing and contract data to produce recurring revenue reports and improve revenue forecasting accuracy.
skills.evidence_quote: you can use this feature to set up automated contracts, which will supply you with recurring revenue reports. As a result, you'll be able to forecast more accurately.

skills.definition: Configure invoicing processes including invoice consolidation, multi-schedule billing, and flexible pricing to satisfy customer-specific billing requirements.
skills.evidence_quote: if the customer has multiple billing schedules, you can use recurring contract billing to consolidate their invoices.

extraction.notes: Subscription Management, Revenue Operations, and Billing and Invoicing all coexist at résumé granularity; kept separate per default-to-include.

module_name: Explore device encryption features

skills.definition: Configure and manage BitLocker drive encryption to protect data on Windows devices against unauthorized access when storage media are removed.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the benefits of drive encryption with BitLocker.

microsoft_surface_forms: encryption to protect drives and files
skills.definition: Apply file- and drive-level encryption techniques to protect data at rest from unauthorized access beyond OS permissions.
skills.evidence_quote: Encryption provides an extra layer of protection against unauthorized access, when permissions alone are insufficient.

skills.definition: Understand and leverage TPM hardware to underpin secure key storage and platform integrity for device encryption.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the benefits of a Trusted Platform Module (TPM).

microsoft_surface_forms: protect drives and files against unauthorized access
skills.definition: Apply encryption and platform security controls to harden Windows endpoint devices against data exposure.
skills.evidence_quote: This module explains the benefits of using encryption to protect drives and files against unauthorized access.

extraction.notes: Endpoint Security included as the broader parent skill alongside the specific BitLocker/TPM/EFS components.

module_name: Evaluate costs and benefits of AI solutions

skills.definition: Assesses AI initiatives against business objectives and decides where AI delivers sustainable, value-driven impact.
skills.evidence_quote: comprehensive framework for assessing, justifying, and selecting AI-powered business solutions

skills.definition: Quantifies the full lifecycle cost of a technology investment including acquisition, operation, and maintenance.
skills.evidence_quote: Select ROI Criteria for AI-Powered Business Solutions, Including Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)

skills.definition: Defines and calculates return-on-investment criteria to justify technology spending against measurable business value.
skills.evidence_quote: foundational concepts of Return on Investment (ROI) and Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)

skills.definition: Designs end-to-end technology solutions and makes build-vs-buy-vs-extend decisions for business capabilities.
skills.evidence_quote: deciding whether to build, buy, or extend AI components for business processes

skills.definition: Builds quantitative and qualitative justification packages for technology investments aimed at executive stakeholders.
skills.evidence_quote: equip solution architects and business decision-makers with a comprehensive framework for assessing, justifying, and selecting

extraction.notes: Vendor-neutral evaluation discipline; AI Strategy included to capture the AI-specific lens vs. generic IT investment analysis.

module_name: Measure and mitigate risks for a generative AI app in Azure AI Foundry

skills.definition: Builds, evaluates, and operates generative AI applications on Microsoft's Azure AI Foundry platform.
skills.evidence_quote: create a chatbot in [Azure AI Foundry](https://ai.azure.com)

skills.definition: Applies practices and tooling to identify, measure, and mitigate harms in AI systems before and after deployment.
skills.evidence_quote: measure and mitigate risks for a generative AI app leveraging responsible AI tools and features

skills.definition: Designs and ships LLM-powered applications including chatbots that respond grounded in enterprise data.
skills.evidence_quote: create a chatbot in [Azure AI Foundry] ... that can generate responses about Contoso Camping Store product features

microsoft_surface_forms: Upload data and create an index
skills.definition: Grounds LLM outputs in retrieved enterprise content via indexes and vector stores.
skills.evidence_quote: Upload data and create an index

skills.definition: Writes and tunes system and user prompts to steer LLM behavior toward desired outcomes.
skills.evidence_quote: Set up a system message

skills.definition: Designs and runs evaluations of AI system outputs using human review and LLM-as-judge methods.
skills.evidence_quote: Execute a manual evaluation ... Execute and analyze an AI-assisted evaluation

skills.definition: Configures filters and policies to block harmful or off-policy content in AI applications.
skills.evidence_quote: Create and add a content filter

extraction.notes: Defaulted-to-include RAG and Prompt Engineering — each backed by a single learning objective but represent distinct résumé-grade competencies.

module_name: Evaluate Windows client installation methods

skills.definition: Plans and executes installation and deployment of Windows client operating systems across user and machine scenarios.
skills.evidence_quote: different processes and methods for installing Windows 10 and 11

skills.definition: Manages the lifecycle of end-user devices including provisioning, upgrades, and replacement.
skills.evidence_quote: whether you're deploying for a new or existing user, replacing a machine, or upgrading the OS

skills.definition: Installs, configures, and maintains desktop operating systems.
skills.evidence_quote: Understand the process of installing the Windows client

microsoft_surface_forms: methods of activation for the Windows client
skills.definition: Manages product activation and licensing compliance for client operating systems.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the methods of activation for the Windows client

module_name: Explore release strategy recommendations

skills.definition: Designs and governs the process of moving software changes from build to production through staged, approved releases.
skills.evidence_quote: critical release strategy recommendations for designing automated deployment workflows

skills.definition: Builds continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines that automate testing and deployment of software.
skills.evidence_quote: release strategy recommendations for automated deployments

skills.definition: Uses Microsoft Azure DevOps to manage source, pipelines, and release workflows for software delivery.
skills.evidence_quote: Some exercises require an Azure DevOps Organization and Team Project

skills.definition: Applies practices that bridge development and operations to deliver software reliably and frequently.
skills.evidence_quote: Understanding of what DevOps is and its concepts

skills.definition: Implements automated quality gates and human approval steps that control progression through deployment stages.
skills.evidence_quote: Implement and configure release gates for quality assurance

module_name: Build enterprise AI agents with Java and Spring

skills.definition: Use the Spring AI framework's ChatClient, Advisors API, and embeddings abstractions to integrate LLM-powered features into Java applications.
skills.evidence_quote: Spring AI is a powerful framework that simplifies the integration of AI capabilities into Java applications.

skills.definition: Build server-side applications in Java, typically using the Spring ecosystem for configuration, dependency injection, and web/service patterns.
skills.evidence_quote: build an AI-powered application and AI agents, using Spring AI

skills.definition: Provision and call hosted OpenAI models (chat, embeddings) through the Azure OpenAI Service from application code.
skills.evidence_quote: build an AI-powered application and AI agents, using Spring AI with Azure OpenAI

skills.definition: Deploy and operate containerized application workloads on Azure's serverless Container Apps platform.
skills.evidence_quote: deploy a Java application and AI agents using Spring AI, Azure OpenAI, and Azure Container Apps

skills.definition: Design LLM applications that ground responses by retrieving relevant context from a knowledge store before generation.
skills.evidence_quote: Advisors API: encapsulates generative AI patterns - for example, to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

skills.definition: Store and query high-dimensional embeddings for semantic search, using vector-capable stores such as pgvector on PostgreSQL.
skills.evidence_quote: Vector storage integration: built-in support for vector databases, such as `pgvector`.

skills.definition: Design and build LLM-driven agents that combine tools, memory, and reasoning to complete enterprise tasks.
skills.evidence_quote: Build and deploy a Java application and AI agents using Spring AI

extraction.notes: Included both Spring AI and broader Java Development since the module trains both the framework and general Java/Spring patterns.

module_name: Manage device authentication

skills.definition: Administer identities, devices, and authentication policies in Microsoft's cloud identity platform (formerly Azure AD).
skills.evidence_quote: learn about device authentication and management in Microsoft Entra ID

skills.definition: Register, join, and govern devices as first-class identities in a cloud directory so they can be used as a security signal and managed centrally.
skills.evidence_quote: a device is becoming another identity you want to protect... Microsoft Entra ID allows you to join Windows based devices, as well as to manage them

skills.definition: Govern how users and devices authenticate, are authorized, and access organizational resources.
skills.evidence_quote: Besides managing user authentication and protecting user identities, it's also important to cover device authentication

module_name: Create an Azure Content Understanding client application

skills.definition: Use Azure's multimodal Content Understanding service to build analyzers that extract structured information from documents, images, audio, and video.
skills.evidence_quote: Azure Content Understanding is a multimodal service that simplifies the creation of AI-powered analyzers that can extract information from multiple content formats

skills.definition: Write client code that calls remote HTTP/REST services, handling authentication, request shaping, and response parsing.
skills.evidence_quote: develop client applications that use Azure Content Understanding analyzers by using the Python SDK or the REST API

skills.definition: Build applications in Python, including consuming vendor SDKs to integrate with cloud services.
skills.evidence_quote: write code that uses the Python SDK and REST API

skills.definition: Apply AI techniques that jointly process multiple content modalities (text, images, audio, video) to extract information.
skills.evidence_quote: Use the Azure Content Understanding API for multimodal content analysis and information extraction.

skills.definition: Pull structured fields and entities out of unstructured content such as documents, media files, or transcripts.
skills.evidence_quote: extract information from multiple content formats, including documents, images, audio files, and videos

module_name: Guided project: Monitor Azure with Service Health and Activity Log alerts

skills.definition: Configure and operate Azure's native monitoring service to detect platform incidents, management operations, and resource changes.
skills.evidence_quote: Azure Monitor provides several alert types that notify you about issues without requiring paid compute resources.

skills.definition: Establish proactive monitoring baselines for cloud environments, including alerting on platform health and operational events.
skills.evidence_quote: This guided project helps you build a starter monitoring baseline using free Azure alerting services.

skills.definition: Design alert rules and notification routing (action groups, email) so the right people get paged when systems misbehave or critical resources change.
skills.evidence_quote: You set up an action group with email notification, create a Service Health alert for platform incidents, and create an Activity Log alert for resource deletions.

skills.definition: Operate resources in the Microsoft Azure cloud, including configuring platform-level services.
skills.evidence_quote: Your operations team wants to know immediately when Azure platform issues occur or when someone deletes a critical resource.

extraction.notes: Kept Azure Monitor and generic Cloud Monitoring as coexisting skills at the same granularity, per rule on Windows Containers vs Containerization.

module_name: Describe monitoring tools in Azure

skills.definition: Use Azure Monitor to collect metrics and logs and configure alerts across Azure resources.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe Azure Monitor, including Azure Log Analytics, Azure Monitor Alerts, and Application Insights.

skills.definition: Query and analyze log data collected from cloud and on-premises sources for troubleshooting and reporting.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe Azure Monitor, including Azure Log Analytics

skills.definition: Instrument applications to capture performance, availability, and usage telemetry for diagnosing live-service issues.
skills.evidence_quote: Azure Monitor Alerts, and Application Insights.

skills.definition: Use cloud-vendor advisor tooling to surface cost, security, reliability, and performance recommendations across an environment.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the purpose of Azure Advisor.

skills.definition: Track platform-provider health events and translate them into operational responses for affected services.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe Azure Service Health.

skills.definition: Operate and monitor workloads on the Microsoft Azure cloud platform.
skills.evidence_quote: tools that help you monitor your environment and applications, both in Azure and in on-premises or multicloud environments.

extraction.notes: Module is descriptive/introductory; surfaced one skill per named tool since each is a discrete competency listed in learning objectives.

module_name: Understand Microsoft Purview eDiscovery

skills.definition: Run defensible legal-discovery processes to identify, preserve, collect, review, and produce electronically stored information for investigations.
skills.evidence_quote: Organizations need a consistent, defensible process for identifying, preserving, reviewing, and producing electronically stored information (ESI).

skills.definition: Use the Microsoft Purview compliance suite to manage investigations, holds, searches, and review across Microsoft 365.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Purview provides eDiscovery tools that help legal, compliance, and security teams manage this process end to end.

skills.definition: Place and manage holds on electronic content so it cannot be altered or deleted during litigation or investigation.
skills.evidence_quote: preserving content, running searches, analyzing results

skills.definition: Govern enterprise content lifecycle — search, classification, review, and access — to meet regulatory and investigative obligations.
skills.evidence_quote: With support for role-based access control, content search, legal holds, review sets, and advanced analytics

skills.definition: Design and apply RBAC to scope user access to sensitive workflows like eDiscovery cases and review sets.
skills.evidence_quote: controlling access through role-based permissions

skills.definition: Respond to regulatory and legal requests with defensible, repeatable processes across enterprise data.
skills.evidence_quote: Legal investigations, regulatory requests, and internal reviews often require more than just finding documents.

extraction.notes: Defaulted to include RBAC and Regulatory Compliance — both are clearly trained but are adjacent rather than the main subject.
